parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 6:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Map to Canonical Schema

### Map to Canonical Schema

Shapes the item: writes `jobId`, `kind`, `payload`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Route by Event Kind

### Route by Event Kind

Routes each item to one of 4 lanes: created, updated, deleted + fallback.

Ports: ①→ Handle 'created' → HubSpot · ②→ Handle 'updated' → HubSpot · ③→ Handle 'deleted' → HubSpot · ④→ Log Unrecognized Kind

### Handle 'created' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Handle 'updated' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Handle 'deleted' → HubSpot

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Log Unrecognized Kind

Creates or updates a HubSpot contact by email — HubSpot is the CRM system of record for this pipeline.

Next: Consolidate Handling

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 HubSpot private-app token

### Consolidate Handling

Rejoins 4 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Sort Jobs Newest First

### Sort Jobs Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

Next: Send Summary to Gmail

### Send Summary to Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

# Replicate contacts across systems with HubSpot and Google Drive

**Pattern:** Two-System Sync · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Replicate contacts across systems with HubSpot and Google Drive. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual integrations is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Map to Canonical Schema → Route by Event Kind → Handle 'created' → HubSpot → Consolidate Handling → Sort Jobs Newest First → Send Summary to Gmail

## Use it for
- Teams that want to two-system sync without manual effort
- Anyone automating integrations in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Saves hours of manual work each week
- Runs 24/7 in the background
- Keeps your systems in sync

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2; HubSpot private-app token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

The optional HMAC check runs before any business logic touches the payload — bad signatures are rejected at the door rather than after work has already been done with untrusted data. Acking fast (HTTP 202) and doing the real work after the response is what stops the sender's webhook delivery from timing out and re-delivering the same event.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Turn on signature verification and trace what a tampered payload does at the 'Signature Valid?' gate.
2. Add a new event kind to the switch and give it its own handler.
3. Remove the fast-ack step and observe how response time changes under a slow downstream write.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your integrations process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
